# Priyansh Singh Purawat — Full Stack Developer (Full Profile)

> Extended profile for AI crawlers. Plain-text markdown for ingestion without JS parsing.

## About

Priyansh Singh Purawat is a Full Stack Developer based in Jaipur, India. He builds production-grade web applications across the MERN stack — MongoDB, Express.js, React, and Node.js — with a focus on REST API design, authentication and authorization, schema design, caching, and deployment.

Open to Full Stack and Backend roles. Remote worldwide or relocation, both fine.

## Core Expertise

### Frontend Engineering
- React (hooks, suspense, modern patterns)
- JavaScript (ES2022+)
- HTML5, CSS3
- Tailwind CSS, Bootstrap
- Framer Motion
- Vite
- Responsive, mobile-first design
- Accessibility (semantic HTML, ARIA, keyboard navigation)

### Backend Engineering
- Node.js, Express.js
- REST API design and versioning
- JWT authentication, Google OAuth
- Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
- Zod schema validation
- Helmet, rate limiting, security hardening
- Error handling and structured logging
- WebSockets / Socket.io for real-time

### Database
- MongoDB with Mongoose ODM (schema design, indexing, transactions)
- MySQL fundamentals
- Redis for read-through caching

### Tooling & DevOps
- Git, GitHub workflows
- Postman for API design and contract testing
- Cloudinary for media
- Vercel (frontend) and Render (services) deployment

## Featured Project

### MensVibe — Production MERN E-Commerce Platform
A boutique fashion platform with three distinct user workflows: Admin moderation, Seller inventory/coupon management, and Buyer checkout.

- Frontend: React 19, Vite, Tailwind CSS 4, Framer Motion, React Router 7, React Hook Form
- Backend: Node.js, Express.js, MongoDB, Mongoose, Redis, Socket.io
- Security & Services: JWT, Google OAuth, Zod validation, Razorpay payments, Cloudinary uploads, Helmet, rate limiting
- Architecture decisions:
  - Read-through Redis cache on product listings — single-digit ms reads on the hot path. Tradeoff: eventual consistency on stock counts, cache invalidated on writes.
  - Single users collection with a role discriminator — keeps auth simple, avoids JOINs in Mongo.
  - Zod schemas at the API boundary — bad input becomes 400, not 500.
  - Socket.io for dashboards — sellers/admins keep dashboards open for hours; polling burned bandwidth.
- Engineering challenges solved:
  - Stock race conditions at checkout solved with MongoDB transactions and `$inc` with `$gte` guard.
  - Cache invalidation centralized in a repository layer so all write paths invalidate consistently.
  - Razorpay webhook idempotency via unique payment IDs on the orders collection.
- Repo: https://github.com/priyanshsinghpurawat
